ops.title: Wrap SDK errors at optional-dependency boundaries
ops.body: The retrain extra's SDKs (tinker) must never leak their exception types across module boundaries: consumers that only mock or classify failures would need `import tinker` just to name the catch class, and CI runs the suite WITHOUT the retrain extra (the repo contract — retrain logic is tested with mocked SDK calls and lazy imports). The pattern, established by d0bc7fe6: the module that genuinely requires the SDK (cc_steer/retrain/tinker.py) converts SDK errors to a domain exception at its own call sites (`except tinker.TinkerError as error: raise TinkerCallError(...) from error`, TinkerCallError subclassing RuntimeError), and every consumer catches the domain type with no SDK import. Apply the same shape to any future optional-dependency integration; a consumer-side `import <sdk>` for a catch tuple is the bug signature (it passed every local gate because dev venvs carry the extra — only the no-extra CI lane caught it).
